revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Higashi-Chōda Kofun group]][[ja:東町田墳墓群]]
{{Short description|Yayoi period Kofuns in Ōgaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Higashi-Chōda Kofun group | native_name =東町田墳墓群 | native_name_lang = | image = Higashi chōda Tumuli, Ogaki, 2019.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Higashi-Chōda Kofun group | map_type = Japan Gifu Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Higashi-Chōda Kofun group | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|22|49|N|136|34|10|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki, Gifu]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 5,107 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 3rd to early 4th century | abandoned = | epochs ={{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (No public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }}
{{nihongo|'''Higashi-Chōda Kofun group'''|東町田墳墓群|Higashi-Chōda funbo-gun}} is a cluster of {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} [[kofun|Kofun]]s located in the Hirui neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Ōgaki, Gifu|Ōgaki]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/377467|title=東町田墳墓群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref> The site is important as it covers a transitional period between the Yayoi and the later [[Kofun period]].
==Overview== The Higashi-Chōda Kofun Group is located on a {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terrace|1=Fluvial terrace}} at an elevation of 15 to 18 meters at the foot of Mount Kinsei in the northwestern part of Ōgaki. The area has been inhabited from the [[Jōmon period]] through the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} as it is terrains which is resistant to flood damage. Two kilometers to the southeast is the Arao Minami Ruins, which was a contemporary settlement with a double moat. Nine Kofuns and Kofuns have been confirmed so far, which were built from the end of the Yayoi period to the early [[Kofun period]]. These include two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (''hōfun'' (方墳)), two {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round ''kofun''|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (''empun'' (円墳)) in close proximity (which date from the Yayoi period), and to the east, two "cojoined-rectangle" style keyhole ''kofun'' ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}) and three more {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=square ''kofun''|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (which date from the very end of the Yayoi period to the start of the Kofun period). No ''[[fukiishi]]'' or ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, but traces of moats remain. All of the tombs area relatively small, with the square ''kofun'' less than 10 meters on each side. No trace of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s have survived.
The site was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2010 through 2014. A large amount of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|qid=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} was recovered, some depicting people, gabled stilt buildings, deer and other animals. Other items included stone mills coated with [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]]. These artifacts are designated an Ōgaki City Important Cultural Property and are displayed at the {{nihongo|'''Ōgaki City Museum of History and Folklore'''|大垣市歴史民俗資料館|Ōgaki-shi rekishi minzoku shiryōkan}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|qid=Q6573261|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gifu)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.ogaki.lg.jp/0000037024.html Ogaki City guide] {{in lang|ja}}
